status: Editorial draft v1 — illustrative and evidence-informed; not a sector-ratified standard.
note: Point values, indicative RPL hours and provenance are NTLSN's own model, published openly so recognition is transparent and comparable. They are NOT an Advance HE, AQF, TEQSA or Universities Australia equivalence or endorsement. Institutions decide any credit. Versioned and open for comment.
cite: NTLSN (2026). Recognition Credit Framework v1.0. National Teaching & Learning Scholarship Network. https://ntlsn.com/recognition-framework.html

moves.label: Redesign a class, tutorial or lab using an evidence-based approach
moves.evidences: Design rationale plus a before/after reflection

moves.label: Adopt active, flipped or inquiry-based learning across a unit
moves.evidences: The redesign and a reflection on its effect on engagement

moves.label: Teach in a new mode (online, hybrid or work-integrated)
moves.evidences: Materials and a reflection on the mode shift

moves.label: Be peer-observed and act on the feedback
moves.evidences: The observation record and what you changed

moves.label: Design or substantially revise a unit or subject
moves.evidences: The unit outline and design rationale

moves.label: Map and align learning outcomes across a unit (constructive alignment)
moves.evidences: An alignment map of outcomes, activities and assessment

moves.label: Embed inclusive / Universal Design for Learning into a unit
moves.evidences: The inclusive design choices and their rationale

moves.label: Co-design curriculum or assessment with students (Students as Partners)
moves.evidences: The partnership process and outcome

moves.label: Redesign an assessment for authenticity or AI-resilience
moves.evidences: The task and the design reasoning

moves.label: Build or calibrate a criterion-referenced marking rubric
moves.evidences: The rubric and any calibration notes

moves.label: Run or take part in assessment moderation or calibration
moves.evidences: The moderation record and agreed actions

moves.label: Improve feedback practice (e.g. dialogic or feed-forward)
moves.evidences: The change and evidence students acted on it

moves.label: Read and apply SoTL literature to change your practice
moves.evidences: What you read and how practice changed

moves.label: Gather and analyse evidence of your teaching's impact
moves.evidences: The evidence and your analysis

moves.label: Present teaching work at a seminar, symposium or conference
moves.evidences: The abstract or slides and the venue

moves.label: Publish peer-reviewed scholarship of teaching and learning
moves.evidences: The publication

moves.label: Complete a teaching qualification or professional-development program
moves.evidences: The certificate or completion record

moves.label: Gain an HEA fellowship (AFHEA, FHEA, SFHEA or PFHEA)
moves.evidences: The fellowship award

moves.label: Receive a teaching award or citation (institutional or national)
moves.evidences: The award or citation

moves.label: Be nominated or shortlisted for a teaching award
moves.evidences: The nomination

moves.label: Mentor a colleague or new sessional in their teaching
moves.evidences: The mentoring arrangement and outcome

moves.label: Lead a teaching project, special-interest group or community of practice
moves.evidences: Your leadership role and what it achieved

moves.label: Contribute to teaching & learning governance or committees
moves.evidences: The role and your contribution

moves.label: Serve as an external examiner or peer reviewer of teaching
moves.evidences: The review undertaken
